message:
feat(extensions): policy-mode-aware quota defaults, breach telemetry, subprocess lifecycle tracking, and comprehensive quota unit tests (SEC-4.1)

Quota Config Tiered Defaults
------------------------------
Replace the single hardcoded `ExtensionQuotaConfig::default()` with
`ExtensionQuotaConfig::for_mode(ExtensionPolicyMode)` that produces
tier-appropriate limits:

- Strict: 20/s burst, 500/min sustained, 5K total cap, 4 subprocesses,
  50 MB write budget, 200 HTTP requests
- Prompt (baseline): 100/s burst, 2K/min sustained, no total cap,
  8 subprocesses, no write/HTTP caps
- Permissive: 500/s burst, 10K/min sustained, no total cap,
  32 subprocesses, no write/HTTP caps

The default() impl delegates to for_mode(Prompt) for backwards compat.

Quota Breach Telemetry (QuotaBreachEvent)
-------------------------------------------
Add `QuotaBreachEvent` struct emitted whenever a quota limit is
breached, recording timestamp, extension_id, capability, reason, and
quota_config_source ("per_extension" or "global"). Events are stored
in a bounded VecDeque on ExtensionManagerInner and drainable via
`drain_quota_breach_events()` for downstream telemetry consumers.
`quota_breach_count()` provides a non-draining count.

Per-Extension Override Resolution via Policy
----------------------------------------------
`check_quota()` now takes a `&ExtensionPolicy` parameter and resolves
per-extension quota overrides directly from `policy.per_extension[ext_id].quota`
instead of the previous placeholder that always fell back to global
defaults. This makes per-extension quota customization actually functional.

Subprocess Lifecycle Tracking in Dispatch
-------------------------------------------
`dispatch_host_call_shared()` now records subprocess spawn/exit events
around exec capability dispatches:
- Pre-dispatch: `record_subprocess_spawn()` increments counter
- Post-dispatch: `record_subprocess_exit()` decrements counter
This enables accurate concurrent subprocess tracking for the max_subprocesses
quota limit.

Quota State Management
-----------------------
- `reset_quota_state(extension_id)`: clears all counters for an extension
- Formatting fixes: single-line format strings where they fit

Comprehensive Unit Tests (20 new tests)
-----------------------------------------
- for_mode() produces distinct configs per policy tier
- Strict < Prompt < Permissive monotonicity for rate limits
- check_extension_quota: per-second burst, per-minute rate, sliding
  window expiry, total budget, subprocess limit, HTTP request limit,
  write bytes limit
- Manager integration: per-extension override, global default fallback,
  no-ext-id always allowed
- Subprocess spawn/exit tracking with saturating underflow
- Write bytes cumulative tracking
- Breach telemetry recording and draining
- Quota state reset
- Config serialization roundtrip
- Per-extension override policy serialization
- Monotonic total counter (never decreases)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
